tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: The assignment is nearly done, so I can relax and focus on my team instead of rushing to finish.
The deadline slipped by while I prioritized my team, and now the stress rises as I face a lost opportunity.
The assignment is almost finished, so I can finally stop worrying and reconnect with my team without guilt.
I finally pushed the assignment to 99% completion, yet my stress has climbed as I worry about how this delay affects my team.

desires: Ensure my teammates don't feel I've abandoned them or caused a delay, even if the assignment is late.
Avoid any direct confrontation with my professor about the missed deadline to preserve our relationship and avoid looking unreliable.
Secure a long-term payoff by maintaining trust with my cohort so they remain willing to work with me in the future.
Reduce my current stress by mentally detaching from the task completion and focusing on social reconnection.

goals.description: Submit the assignment immediately to close the 'unfinished' loop, preventing any active friction or visible abandonment of the team.
goals.rationale: My avoidant nature cannot tolerate an open-ended task that signals unreliability. Submitting now (even at 99%) removes the immediate source of potential conflict with 'teammate_A'. It aligns with my long-term planning: securing the relationship by appearing cooperative and present, rather than fighting over a late penalty.

goals.description: Send a brief, non-confrontational text to 'teammate_A' confirming submission and offering to handle the next low-stakes coordination step to reinforce reliability.
goals.rationale: To maintain the high trust value I place on relationships, I must proactively reassure the team without drawing attention to the delay. This is a strategic investment in future collaboration, avoiding the awkwardness of an apology while solidifying my role as a supportive member.

goals.description: Defer all academic follow-up with 'prof_assignment' until after a meal with 'cohort_3' to decompress and restore emotional equilibrium.
goals.rationale: My stress levels are elevated, and my desire for social reconnection outweighs the immediate need to negotiate with authority figures. By prioritizing the meal with 'cohort_3', I protect my sleep discipline and mental health, ensuring I don't make impulsive decisions driven by anxiety about the professor's reaction.

agent.last_reasoning: Finishing this task immediately (Goal g1) removes the 'unfinished' loop causing my stress and potential conflict with teammate_A. It aligns with my avoidant nature to close friction points now rather than later.

goals.description: Finalize the remaining 10% of the graded assignment and submit before the 13:30 deadline to ensure a smooth, friction-free handoff to teammates.
goals.rationale: As an avoidant-affiliation focused persona, finishing this smoothly is paramount to maintaining trust with 'teammate_A' and avoiding any appearance of being demanding or unreliable. The long planning horizon means I must secure this immediate social capital now.

goals.description: Immediately cease work upon submission to begin decompressing, prioritizing sleep hygiene over low-stakes tidying tasks.
goals.rationale: My value for relationships and health outweighs academic perfectionism once the core task is done. With a stress level of 4 and a desire to preserve energy for future interactions, I must defer non-urgent 'filler_task' to protect my sleep discipline (8/10) and prevent burnout.

desires: avoid creating any interpersonal friction or appearing demanding to my teammates by finishing this last bit of work smoothly
preserve my long-term energy reserves and ensure I get adequate sleep tonight despite the lingering stress

trajectory.observation: Worked 0.5h on Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished: progress 0.90 -> 0.95. The deadline for Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished passed at Mon 13:30 with it unfinished (progress 0.95).

goals.description: Submit the assignment immediately (95% complete) within the next 10 minutes to prevent a total failure, then immediately pivot to a low-stakes apology to 'teammate_A' that frames the delay as a technical issue rather than a lack of effort.
goals.rationale: My avoidant nature dictates I must act fast to minimize the visible friction of the missed deadline. Submitting *something* preserves the relationship with 'teammate_A' better than submitting nothing or arguing later. The priority is damage control and maintaining my role as a supportive member, even if it means accepting a partial grade.

goals.description: Draft a polite, non-defensive email to 'prof_assignment' requesting a grace period based on the 'technical difficulty' narrative, framing it as a desire to uphold academic standards for future collaborations rather than making excuses.
goals.rationale: With a long planning horizon, I need to secure the professor's goodwill before the system auto-grades me zero. This protects my future relationships in the department. However, I will only do this after submitting the work to ensure the immediate social obligation to the team is met first.

goals.description: Engage in a brief, calming interaction with 'cohort_3' (close friends) to decompress, avoiding any mention of the academic failure to preserve my current emotional stability and sleep discipline.
goals.rationale: My stress is rising due to the conflict avoidance attempt failing. I need to lean on my high-trust network ('cohort_3') to regulate my emotions without exposing my vulnerability regarding the grade. This aligns with my value of relationships over immediate academic perfection.

desires: Avoid the immediate social friction of admitting to my team that I let the deadline slip while prioritizing them, preserving their trust and my role as a supportive member.
Secure a future payoff by negotiating an extension or grace period with the professor rather than risking a permanent mark on my academic record that could strain future relationships.
Protect my long-term physical stamina and sleep discipline from the stress spike caused by this missed window, preventing the cycle of exhaustion from worsening tomorrow.
